questions.question: Which two roles must a user hold to connect a Notion workspace to a GitHub organization?

options.text: Notion workspace owner and GitHub repository collaborator
options.explanation: A repository collaborator isn’t sufficient; you need admin rights on the organization.

options.text: Notion workspace owner and GitHub organization admin
options.explanation: Both ownership of the Notion workspace and admin privileges on the GitHub org are required.

options.text: Notion admin and GitHub organization member
options.explanation: Notion admin isn’t a defined role for this integration, and organization members lack the needed permissions.

options.text: Notion member and GitHub organization owner
options.explanation: Being a Notion member isn’t enough; you must be the workspace owner.

questions.question: What happens if you try to connect a second Notion workspace to the same GitHub organization without disconnecting the first?

options.text: Both workspaces will be connected simultaneously
options.explanation: Only one workspace can be linked to a GitHub organization at a time.

options.text: The connection will fail until you disconnect the existing workspace
options.explanation: You must first disconnect the current workspace before linking another.

options.text: Notion will automatically merge the two workspaces
options.explanation: Merging workspaces isn’t a feature of the integration.

options.text: GitHub will reject the authentication request
options.explanation: The issue is on Notion’s side; GitHub still accepts the request but Notion blocks it.

questions.question: Which of the following GitHub properties are NOT currently supported in Notion synced databases?

options.text: Assignees
options.explanation: Assignees are listed as a supported property.

options.text: Labels and tags
options.explanation: Labels and tags are explicitly noted as unsupported.

options.text: Creator
options.explanation: Creator is included in the supported list.

options.text: State
options.explanation: State is a supported property.

questions.question: When creating a GitHub code preview in Notion, which step ensures the code stays up‑to‑date with future changes?

options.text: Copy the raw file content and paste it as plain text
options.explanation: Plain text won’t update automatically.

options.text: Paste the permalink of a specific commit
options.explanation: A specific commit pins the code to that snapshot, not future changes.

options.text: Paste the permalink of a branch or file line range
options.explanation: Linking to a branch or line range keeps the preview dynamic as the branch updates.

options.text: Upload a PDF of the code snippet
options.explanation: PDFs are static and not linked to GitHub.

questions.question: What is required for identity mapping between GitHub users and Notion profiles to work correctly?

options.text: Enable two‑factor authentication on GitHub
options.explanation: 2FA isn’t related to identity mapping.

options.text: Make sure the GitHub email address is public and add the Notion email to the GitHub profile
options.explanation: Both steps are needed: email must be visible and the Notion email added to the profile.

options.text: Create a separate Notion workspace for each GitHub user
options.explanation: One workspace can map multiple users; separate workspaces aren’t required.

options.text: Install the Notion Chrome extension on the GitHub account
options.explanation: There is no Chrome extension needed for identity mapping.

questions.question: Which property must a Notion database contain before you can add the "GitHub Pull Requests" property?

options.text: Created time
options.explanation: Created time is optional and not a prerequisite for PR linking.

options.text: Unique ID
options.explanation: A Unique ID property is required to match PRs with Notion tasks.

options.text: Assignees
options.explanation: Assignees are a GitHub property but not required for the PR property.

options.text: Status
options.explanation: While useful, status isn’t required to add the PR property.

questions.question: How can you prevent Notion tasks from automatically creating backlinks to GitHub pull requests?

options.text: Delete the GitHub Pull Requests property from the database
options.explanation: Removing the property stops the link entirely, not just backlinks.

options.text: Toggle off "Linkbacks in GitHub" in the property settings
options.explanation: The setting directly controls whether backlinks are created.

options.text: Disable the entire GitHub integration in Notion Settings
options.explanation: That would stop all GitHub features, not just backlinks.

options.text: Set the task status to "Closed" manually
options.explanation: Status changes don’t affect backlink creation.

questions.question: When using magic words in a PR description, which of the following outcomes will mark the linked Notion task as done?

options.text: Using the word "ref" before the task ID
options.explanation: "ref" establishes a reference relationship, not a completion trigger.

options.text: Using any of the words "close", "fix", or "resolve" before the task ID
options.explanation: These words signal that the PR resolves the task, so the task is marked done when merged.

options.text: Including the task ID without any magic word
options.explanation: Without a magic word, the task is only linked, not marked as done.

options.text: Using the phrase "part of" before the task ID
options.explanation: "part of" creates a relationship but does not trigger task completion.

questions.question: Which sequence correctly describes how Notion automatically updates a task’s status based on a linked GitHub Pull Request?

options.text: Select the GitHub Pull Requests property → Edit property → Choose status property → Set mapping for Opened, Review Requested, Approved, Merged
options.explanation: This is the exact workflow described for auto‑updating task status.

options.text: Add a Unique ID property → Paste the PR URL → Notion updates status automatically without further configuration
options.explanation: You still need to configure the auto‑update mapping in the property settings.

options.text: Create a synced database → Enable "Activity Comments" → Status updates happen automatically
options.explanation: "Activity Comments" only shows activity; it doesn’t control status updates.

options.text: Turn on "Linkbacks in GitHub" → Status changes are reflected in Notion tasks
options.explanation: Linkbacks create backlinks, not status synchronization.

questions.question: If you want to stop using the GitHub integration entirely for a workspace, which step is mandatory?

options.text: Remove all GitHub-linked databases from Notion
options.explanation: Databases can remain; the integration must be disconnected at the settings level.

options.text: Disconnect GitHub from Notion via Settings → Connections → Disconnect all users
options.explanation: This is the required procedure to fully disable the integration.

options.text: Delete the Notion workspace
options.explanation: Deleting the workspace is unnecessary and extreme.

options.text: Revoke the GitHub OAuth token manually on GitHub
options.explanation: Revoking the token alone doesn’t update Notion’s connection state.
